arts.ttl: Borussia Dortmund ‘deserved to lose’ after performance in Barcelona – Niko Kovac
arts.sum: After Borussia Dortmund’s 4-0 humiliation in Barcelona, Niko Kovac didn’t mince his words. The Bundesliga side responded well after Raphinha opened the scoring for the Blaugrana midway through the first…

arts.ttl: Emre Can criticises Borussia Dortmund’s performance in Barcelona
arts.sum: Borussia Dortmund will need a miracle if they are to qualify for a place in the UEFA Champions League semi-final after their 4-0 hammering against Barcelona on Tuesday night. Speaking…

arts.ttl: Lamine Yamal Makes Champions League History With Goal vs. Borussia Dortmund
arts.sum: Lamine Yamal became the first player to score multiple Champions League knockout phase goals before turning 18.

arts.ttl: Lamine Yamal: Hansi Flick offers latest update after Barcelona injury scare
arts.sum: Teenage sensation was replaced by Ansu Fati during the latter stages of Champions League demolition against Borussia Dortmund

arts.ttl: MOTM Poll: Borussia Dortmund Outplayed by Hansi Flick’s Barcelona
arts.sum: I actually think that BVB deserved a goal after some strong minutes late on, but it simply wasn’t to be. Barcelona would probably have had at least one more goal if not for Gregor Kobel’s very strong saves in the first half.

arts.ttl: Relieved Raphinha admits he apologized to Cubarsi
arts.sum: Cubarsi thought he’d scored the opening goal after flicking home a header from Inigo Martinez, but Raphinha just got a touch before it crossed the line. Raphinha went on to play a starring role alongside Lamine Yamal and Robert Lewandowski and spoke about this Barcelona team-mates.

arts.ttl: Lewandowski doubles up as Barcelona dominate Dortmund to close on semis
arts.athr: Sid Lowe at the Estadi Olímpic Lluís Companys
arts.sum: There is another game to be played but on this evidence, Barcelona will do so for just the fun of it, and there may be no one having as much fun as they are right now. A semi-final place is virtually secure with a second leg to spare after all three of their fantastic forward line scored on route to a 4-0 victory over Borussia Dortmund at Montjuic, the last of them scored by a kid who even at 17 may have a case to be considered the best player on the continent. Robert Lewandowski, with two, and Raphinha got the others, taking Barcelona to 144 goals this season and almost certainly to the next round and perhaps beyond. They will take some stopping; Dortmund couldn’t do it, certainly. only momentarily did they even get close on a night that began and ended with Barcelona. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Barcelona 4-0 Borussia Dortmund: Player Ratings as Barca Have One Foot in Semifinals
arts.sum: Raphinha, Lamine Yamal and Robert Lewandowski scored to lead Barcelona to a 4–0 win vs. Borussia Dortmund in the Champions League quarterfinals.
